Title: Versatility in acyltransferase activity completes chicoric acid biosynthesis in purple coneflower

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Throughout the plant; stored in vacuoles
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potential antiviral, anti-inflammatory, glucose and lipid homeostatic, neuroprotective, anti-atherosclerosis, and anti-aging activities; quality index for Echinacea products
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BAHD acyltransferase family; HCT-related clade; conserved HXXXD/DFGWG motifs with HHLVD and DFGWG
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Chicoric acid biosynthesis / caffeic acid derivative pathway
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Caftaric acid-forming branch feeding vacuolar chicoric acid synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a caffeoyl group from caffeoyl-CoA to tartaric acid to form caftaric acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes caftaric acid accumulation and thereby supplies precursor for chicoric acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Activity-guided protein purification; peptide MS matched to RNA-seq; recombinant enzyme assay in E. coli; LC-HRMS/UPLC product identification; hairy root overexpression; RNAi silencing; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EpHTT was identified from active hairy-root fractions as a BAHD acyltransferase catalyzing caftaric acid formation. 2. Recombinant EpHTT produced caftaric acid in vitro from caffeoyl-CoA and tartaric acid. 3. Overexpression increased and RNAi decreased caftaric acid in Echinacea hairy roots, confirming in vivo function.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of methyl jasmonate-treated and control hairy roots; SRA: SRR8935731, SRR8935732, SRR8935733, SRR8937034, SRR8937035, SRR8937036
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter in hairy root overexpression; pEAQ-HT-DEST3 transient expression system in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Methyl jasmonate treatment used in candidate discovery; recombinant enzyme assays at pH 7; transient reconstruction in N. benthamiana with tartaric acid feeding
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression manipulated in E. purpurea hairy roots; higher transgene expression correlated with higher caftaric acid
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering chicoric acid production when combined with EpCAS and tartaric acid supply; suitable for pathway reconstruction in plant chassis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: EpHTT likely evolved from the HCT clade through specialization of acyl acceptor recognition; unlike perennial peanut HTT, it prefers caffeoyl-CoA; can use only tartaric acid, not quinic or shikimic acid, as acyl acceptor.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Throughout the plant; stored in vacuoles
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potential antiviral, anti-inflammatory, glucose and lipid homeostatic, neuroprotective, anti-atherosclerosis, and anti-aging activities; quality index for Echinacea products
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BAHD acyltransferase family; HQT clade; conserved HTLSD and DFGWG motifs
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Chicoric acid biosynthesis / chlorogenic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Chlorogenic acid-forming branch feeding vacuolar chicoric acid synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a caffeoyl group from caffeoyl-CoA to quinic acid to form chlorogenic acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes chlorogenic acid accumulation and supports chicoric acid biosynthesis by supplying the acyl donor substrate for EpCAS
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Activity-guided protein purification; peptide MS matched to RNA-seq; recombinant enzyme assay in E. coli; LC-HRMS/UPLC product identification; hairy root overexpression; RNAi silencing; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EpHQT was identified as a BAHD acyltransferase catalyzing chlorogenic acid formation. 2. Recombinant EpHQT produced chlorogenic acid in vitro from caffeoyl-CoA and quinic acid. 3. Overexpression increased and RNAi decreased chlorogenic acid in hairy roots; reduced EpHQT expression also lowered chicoric acid, indicating precursor supply to the final product.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of methyl jasmonate-treated and control hairy roots; SRA: SRR8935731, SRR8935732, SRR8935733, SRR8937034, SRR8937035, SRR8937036
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter in hairy root overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Methyl jasmonate treatment used in candidate discovery; recombinant enzyme assays at pH 7
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression manipulated in E. purpurea hairy roots; higher transgene expression correlated with higher chlorogenic acid
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful with EpHTT and EpCAS for heterologous chicoric acid engineering where chlorogenic acid supply is needed
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: EpHQT can also use shikimic acid as acyl acceptor but prefers quinic acid; chlorogenic acid acts as the acyl donor used by EpCAS in vacuoles.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potential antiviral, anti-inflammatory, glucose and lipid homeostatic, neuroprotective, anti-atherosclerosis, and anti-aging activities; quality index for Echinacea products
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Serine carboxypeptidase-like acyltransferase (SCPL clade IA); conserved Ser-His-Asp catalytic triad; N-terminal vacuolar targeting peptide
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a caffeoyl group from chlorogenic acid to caftaric acid to form chicoric acid and quinic acid in the vacuole
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly synthesizes chicoric acid and determines final product accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Activity-guided purification; peptide MS identification; recombinant expression in yeast; in vitro trans-acylation assay; subcellular localization; pH profiling; overexpression and RNAi in hairy roots; PMSF inhibition; site-directed mutagenesis; docking; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EpCAS is a vacuolar SCPL acyltransferase that synthesizes chicoric acid from caftaric acid and chlorogenic acid. 2. Overexpression increased and RNAi decreased chicoric acid in hairy roots, confirming its in vivo role. 3. EpCAS uniquely prefers chlorogenic acid rather than 1-O-caffeoyl-β-D-glucose as acyl donor, revealing an unusual SCPL catalytic adaptation.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of methyl jasmonate-treated and control hairy roots; SRA: SRR8935731, SRR8935732, SRR8935733, SRR8937034, SRR8937035, SRR8937036
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter in hairy root overexpression; pEAQ-HT-DEST3 transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Acidic conditions favored activity (optimum pH 4); PMSF inhibited activity dose-dependently; transient reconstruction in N. benthamiana with tartaric acid supplementation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression manipulated in E. purpurea hairy roots; higher transgene expression correlated with higher chicoric acid
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Low catalytic turnover reported in vitro; minor hydrolytic side activity releases caffeic acid and can hydrolyze substrates/product
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key terminal gene for engineering chicoric acid production; coexpression with EpHTT in chlorogenic-acid-producing chassis plus tartaric acid supply enabled pathway reconstruction in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: EpCAS activity depends on Ser180-His430-Asp374 catalytic triad; Ser180A abolished activity; can weakly use 1-O-caffeoyl-β-D-glucose, neochlorogenic acid, cryptochlorogenic acid, and 5-O-caffeoylshikimic acid as donors at <1% relative activity; pathway appears unique to Echinacea spp.
